title: Lauren Morris’ CV
text:
Curious and diligent earth scientist.

LinkedIn



Learning more about the world around me.


Geothermal energy, tectonics, hydrology, geochronology, environmental science, geomorphology, glaciology.




Jan 2023 - April 2026 Brigham Young University - Idaho, Rexburg, ID. - Bachelors of Science in Geology - Minor in Geospatial Information Systems - Geology Society President Sept 2024 - April 2026
Geology and GIS coursework: Field Methods, Advanced Field Methods, Igneous & Metamorphic Petrology, Groundwater Hydrology, Geochemistry, Analytical Paleobiology, Sedimentology & Stratigraphy, Geology Career Prep, Mineralogy, Structural Geology, Directed Study, Environmental Geology, Historical Geology, Physical Geology, Applied GIS for STEM, UAS Based Mapping, Remote Sensing, Web & Mobile GIS, and Intro GIS & Spatial Analysis
Other relevant coursework: Calculus I & Multivariable Calculus, Principles of Physics I & II, General Chemistry I & II, Intro to Programming, Data Science Programming, Biostatistics, Advanced Writing & Research



2024 Department Award for Academic Excellence, BYU-I Department of Geology and Environmental Science, Rexburg, ID, USA
2024 Certificate in Geospatial Information Systems Brigham Young University - Idaho, Rexburg, ID, USA
2025 Department Award for Excellence in Research, BYU-I Department of Geology and Environmental Science, Rexburg, ID, USA



Geology Society President • Collaborated with a team of society officers to plan and execute biweekly activities throughout each semester to increase student involvement, wellbeing, and promote the geosciences • Represented the Geology Society at events and meetings including donor banquets, campus fairs, and weekly meetings for the College of Physical Sciences and Engineering Student Success Council
Member of Geological Society of America
Member of AAPG - Active in BYU-I AAPG Student Chapter
Member of AEG



Oct 2021 - Present Accounting clerk, Amblitel, LLC, Louisville, CO and Remote
• Analyzed medical invoices, receipts, and payrolls for 3 surgery centers, ensuring all data was correct and accounted for • Demonstrated quick learning abilities and completed training faster than expected in using software such as Yooz, Sage Intacct, and Microsoft Excel • Adapted to new technologies and programs, including while working remotely for several months
May 2026 - June 2026 Field Assistant, Brigham Young University - Idaho Department of Geology and Environmental Science, Rexburg, ID • Assisted students and faculty in the field for field camp/field methods capstone project • Drove 12 passenger vans with and without trailers, ATVs, and other campus vehicles • Helped manage logistics before, during, and after the students did their field projects
Jan 2024 - April 2026 Teaching Assistant, Brigham Young University - Idaho Department of Geology and Environmental Science, Rexburg, ID • Coordinated with professors and other TAs in teaching Natural Disasters, Applied GIS for STEM, and Sedimentology & Stratigraphy • Tutored students one-on-one and in group, led monthly study sessions, exam reviews,and proctored exams • Managed communications and grading for 30-60 students per semester • Helped with field trip logistics, planning, and driving 12 passenger vans



Computer and GIS skills: Proficient in MacOS and Windows systems, Past5, ArcGIS products, flying Mavic 3 drones, and familiar with Python. Soft skills: communication, conflict resolution, coordinating and planning Geologic skills: Familiar with Brunton compasses, field mapping, reading and creating cross-sections, using petrographic microscopes, independent learning, and academic reading, writing, and presenting
